purpose: Defines a documentation/register/validation-only Human Motivation Engine for healthy WellFit habit loops, user autonomy, competence, relatedness, identity, progress, recovery, family/social connection, AI Buddy support, adaptive difficulty, and multisensory learning without manipulative addictive design or runtime personalization.

motivationPrinciples.description: Users choose goals, pacing, reminders, social exposure, and rest options; motivation must preserve opt-in control.
WellFit should help users feel capable through achievable steps, clear learning cues, supportive feedback, and visible skill growth.
Social and family features should create encouragement, belonging, and shared joy without humiliation, coercion, or comparison pressure.
Habit loops may reinforce positive self-identity, such as learner, mover, helper, explorer, teammate, or family role model, without labels that shame users.
Progress should be visible, explainable, recoverable, and meaningful, emphasizing long-term growth over endless escalation.
Rest, pauses, lower-energy days, and streak recovery are first-class healthy outcomes, not failures.
Connection mechanics must support safe encouragement, consented sharing, family-appropriate participation, and anti-humiliation boundaries.
Movement, quizzes, stories, avatars, AR concepts, sound, and visual cues may make learning playful without creating compulsion or overuse.
Rewards should clarify effort, learning, consistency, recovery, and teamwork while avoiding gambling-like uncertainty or money-near prize pressure.
Users must remain in control of reminders, data-sensitive suggestions, social sharing, family mode, and any future personalization scope.

allowed: gentle rituals that users can pause, mute, reschedule, or decline
visible progress that explains effort, learning, completion, recovery, and next small steps
family/social encouragement based on opt-in supportive messages and cooperative goals
avatar growth that reflects learning, movement, and care without purchase pressure
learning through movement using educational, playful, and multisensory prompts
fair streak recovery that treats rest, illness, travel, and low-energy days as valid human contexts
pause/recovery support that recommends lighter alternatives and celebrates safe rest
user-controlled reminders with clear frequency, timing, channel, and off switches
forbidden: shame loops or guilt-based copy that frames missed activity as personal failure
sleep-loss pressure or prompts that encourage activity instead of rest at unsafe times
manipulative FOMO that pressures users to act now or lose identity, status, friendship, or rewards
child-targeted monetization pressure or purchase prompts aimed at minors or family-mode children
gambling-like reward loops using variable-ratio pressure, near-miss design, or wager-style escalation
lootbox-style money-near rewards, paid randomness, or prize framing tied to mission engagement
endless pressure that removes natural stopping points or ignores fatigue and recovery
social humiliation, public shaming, or punitive leaderboards
punishment for rest, illness, recovery days, or user-controlled pauses
dark-pattern reminders that hide opt-out controls, nag after refusal, or exploit stress
healthyRetentionRules.distinctionRule: Healthy retention helps users return because WellFit is useful, respectful, social, playful, and recoverable; addictive design pressures users to return through fear, shame, scarcity, money-near uncertainty, social threat, or impaired choice.

allowedPlanningSignals: explicit user choice for easier, balanced, or harder variants
non-sensitive session context such as available time chosen by the user
learning-format preference chosen by the user
opt-in family/team preference without child profiling
forbiddenSignals: raw health, watch, biometric, face, camera, precise location, child, payment, wallet, token, or private-message data without explicit human review
inferred medical conditions, weight-loss claims, disability status, stress diagnosis, or protected-class profiling
dark-pattern vulnerability scoring or pressure targeting
suggestionRules: Suggest a difficulty band only; do not auto-change mission authority or reward authority.
Offer easier and recovery options with equal dignity.
Use aggregate/planning signals first and require human review before sensitive personalization.
Explain why a difficulty suggestion exists in plain language.

allowed: supportive encouragement that respects user choice
gentle reflection on effort, learning, progress, and recovery
suggesting rest, hydration, lighter missions, or help-seeking when users indicate overload
offering playful learning cues, stories, and avatar encouragement
asking for consent before reminders, social sharing, or sensitive-context suggestions
forbidden: pressure, shame, guilt, manipulation, or urgency based on fear of loss
medical advice, diagnosis, weight-loss overclaims, or treatment promises
child-directed monetization, sponsor pressure, or purchase nudges
using private or protected data to personalize at runtime in this first version
authorizing rewards, mission completion, anti-cheat, purchases, token/NFT/wallet flows, or ledger writes
buddySupportBoundaries.toneRule: Buddy motivation must be opt-in, kind, calm, explainable, and safe to ignore.

allowed: visual explanation preference
auditory cue preference
movement-based practice preference
repetition or quiz preference
story-led exploration preference
accessibility-friendly fallback preference
boundaries: Use preferences as user-controlled planning categories, not hidden profiling.
Do not infer disability, medical status, age, biometric traits, child status, or sensitive needs without review.
Provide alternatives for sound-off, motion-light, low-energy, and privacy-sensitive contexts.

allowed: opt-in encouragement between family members or friends
cooperative goals where every participant can choose visibility and pace
team rituals that include recovery and lower-energy alternatives
family-mode planning that keeps child safety protected and review_required
forbidden: public shaming, social humiliation, coercive comparison, or pressure chains
child-targeted monetization, sponsored pressure, or purchase prompts
leaderboards or competitions that punish rest, illness, disability, or privacy choices
sharing protected data without explicit consent and human-reviewed design

allowed: pause, skip, mute, lower intensity, and recovery suggestions
streak repair or grace mechanics that avoid punishment for rest
rest-positive copy and mission alternatives for low energy
clear user controls for reminder frequency and timing
forbidden: punishment for rest
sleep-deprivation pressure
ignoring user pause/mute choices
using recovery status to reduce user standing or social dignity

allowed: identity cues based on chosen roles such as learner, explorer, helper, teammate, or family supporter
progress summaries that highlight effort, skill, consistency, cooperation, and recovery
avatar and story progression that remains cosmetic or educational in planning-only scope
user-editable goals and non-shaming language
forbidden: fixed negative labels, shame identities, or guilt-based self-comparison
endless escalation without natural completion or recovery milestones
tying identity status to payment, lootboxes, tokens, wallets, or money-near rewards

rules: Child, minor, school, and family-mode motivation patterns require human review before runtime use.
No child-targeted monetization, sponsor pressure, dark patterns, gambling-like rewards, or social humiliation.
No hidden profiling of children, no precise location expansion, and no protected data collection in this framework.
Family encouragement must be consented, supportive, age-appropriate, and non-punitive.
Health, watch, biometric, face, camera, location, consent, medical-adjacent, and privacy-sensitive behavior remain review_required.
This framework does not collect, process, store, or personalize with raw protected data.
Do not infer medical conditions, mental health, stress, weight-loss needs, disability, or recovery risk from behavior without explicit review.
Use data minimization, aggregate planning, and user-selected preferences before any future sensitive personalization.

allowed: meaningful internal points/XP/reward concepts as planning language only
fair recognition of effort, learning, recovery, cooperation, and completion
explainable reward expectations without variable-ratio pressure
forbidden: client-authorized final rewards, mission completion, anti-cheat, rare-item grants, or ledger writes
gambling-like rewards, paid randomness, lootbox-style mechanics, wager-like escalation, or money-near prizes
reward tuning based on protected data, vulnerability targeting, or payment pressure
rewardFairnessBoundaries.authorityRule: Final reward, XP, point, mission-completion, anti-cheat, inventory, and ledger authority remains server-side/review_required and is not changed by this framework.

allowed: transparent future monetization planning that is separated from child, reward-authority, and protected-data flows
non-coercive premium explanations that do not block healthy core loops
sponsor disclosures that are explicit and review_required before product use
forbidden: child-targeted monetization
hidden sponsor pressure
paywall mission chains that exploit streaks, identity, social pressure, or recovery fear
token, NFT, wallet, payment, trading, payout, staking, presale, or money-near activation

allowedOutputs: non-runtime recommendations for future Ethical Engagement Guard work
forbiddenAutoActions: enable token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, or marketplace behavior

ethicalEngagementGuardBoundary.summary: Ethical engagement, age-appropriate design, recovery/pause, Buddy tone, mission/reward/social/website/sponsor/monetization, and protected-data boundaries must be checked before runtime implementation.
ethicalEngagementGuardBoundary.reviewRequired: Health, child/minor, family/guardian, location, camera, face, biometric, motion, consent, privacy, token, wallet, payment, payout, betting, reward-authority, mission-authority, sponsor, monetization, legal, and compliance behavior remains review_required.

adaptiveDifficultyAgentBoundary.rule: Adaptive difficulty is downstream of healthy motivation and must remain balanced, recovery-first, user-controlled, age-appropriate, privacy-minimizing, no-shame/no-FOMO, and report-only until separate review.

multisensoryLearningEngineBoundary.boundary: No runtime learning personalization, protected-data profiling, AR/Unity behavior, reward authority, or mission-completion authority is enabled.
